messages.content: I need help of an expert google sheets script writer, and I know you are the best.  In the active sheet the script should set the background color to #DFE3E7 in columns C, H, M, and R and to #D3D3DF" in columns D, I, N, and S. However it should not make any changes in rows where the cell in the A column has text that ends with "day" or "ime" or "of".  If possible the script should be as fast as possible.

The previous script worked great, but it reset the colors in all the other columns to white, which I did not want. Please edit the script so that it does not affect any columns besides the ones I named.

Great, that worked! Now I want you to modify the script to do one more thing: In columns D, I, N, and S, I want it to delete every value, unless one cell to the right (so in the same row but in column C, H, M, and R), there is a value that is at least one character long. If there is, I want the script to not make any edits. It should only delete values in columns  D, I, N, and S if they are to the right of a cell that does not have at least one character. Please also make an effort to keep the script running as fast as possible, like the expert programmer you are!
Oh no, this scrip completely ruined all the formulas on my spreadsheet! Please rewrite it so that only affects the ranges I specified and makes no changes to any cells outside those ranges!

I got the following error:
TypeError: SpreadsheetApp.newTextStyle(...).copy is not a function
setConditionalBackgroundColors	@ Code.gs:162

Somehow it didn't work, so let's start from the beginning. What is a script that would perform the same operation as Google Sheets menu item Format > Clear Formatting on cells in the range H6:H10? Please write this script and call it "clearFromatting".

That worked perfectly. Now I want you to extend the range of that script to include the entire columns C, H, M, and R of the active spreadsheet, but to leave out the cells in rows where the text in column A ends with either "day" or "ime" or " of"
